div1.entryFree:
Fumble, 1) intr. to make awkward endeavours to do any thing: I saw him f. with the sheets, H5 II, 3, 14. with a palsy --ing on his gorget, Troil. I, 3, 174. thou --st, Ant. IV, 4, 14.
2) tr. to handle or manage awkwardly: what dost thou wrap and f. in thine arms? Tit. IV, 2, 58. as many farewells . . . he --s up into a loose adieu, Troil. IV, 4, 48.
